mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce household income and consumer spending, driving negative equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand, forcing corporate earnings revisions downward and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 20-day returns, with a positive return frequency exceeding 50% in triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production levels reflect stronger manufacturing output and supply chain efficiency, boosting corporate revenue expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases do not correlate with positive 5-day returns, yielding a Rank IC below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 290, rising interest rates signal effective monetary tightening that anchors inflation expectations, reducing macroeconomic uncertainty and driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive returns do not materialize in high-inflation regimes when rates rise, with forward returns averaging negative or zero during CPIAUCSL > 290 periods.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll additions above 250,000 demonstrate robust labor demand and wage growth, strengthening consumer purchasing power and corporate profit forecasts to produce positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Payrolls above 250,000 fail to predict positive 20-day returns, with a negative return frequency exceeding 55% in triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above trend expands aggregate corporate profit pools, providing a fundamental earnings tailwind that sustains positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01 over 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized increase in industrial production alongside a declining unemployment rate confirms synchronized economic strengthening, validating earnings growth models and generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Synchronized INDPRO and UNRATE improvements do not generate positive 5-day returns, with a negative return frequency exceeding 50% in triggered periods.
